  "sinks": "The code writes output by rendering an EJS template to HTML, which is then returned as a static page. No untrusted data is written directly to system files or network; the code outputs are primarily static HTML content.",
  "flows": "Data from Hexo posts is processed in 'resolv()' to generate a JSON object representing nodes and links, which is then injected into the EJS template. The template rendering is the final sink. No user input flows directly into system commands or network calls within this code.",
  "anomalies": "No suspicious hardcoded credentials, backdoors, or malicious code are evident. The code does use 'require' for multiple modules, but all are common dependencies. The 'resolv()' function processes post data to generate visualization data without any unusual behavior. The code does not perform any network calls or system modifications beyond file reading and template rendering. No obfuscated or minified code detected.",
  "analysis": "The code loads dependencies for HTTP requests, XML parsing, templating, and logging. It initializes Hexo, loads posts, and constructs a network graph based on post tags and categories. The 'resolv()' function processes post data to generate nodes and links for visualization, returning a JSON object. The main function checks configuration, measures execution time, logs info, and renders an EJS template with parameters derived from configuration and processed data. The output is a static HTML page with embedded JSON data. No dynamic execution, network calls, or sensitive data handling are present. All data flows are controlled and sanitized, and no suspicious or malicious behavior is detected.",
